subject: Need to send this back
body: The jacket fits poorly and I want to return it for a different size. Order ORD-3101.
policy_snippet: Returns can be reviewed for delivered items when the customer provides an order number and return reason.
notes: Should stay a return request and not shift into refund just because the customer is unhappy.

subject: Want my money back
body: My package showed up a week late and I want a refund because I had to buy the item somewhere else. Order ORD-3102.
policy_snippet: Refund requests for delayed delivery require review of order details and whether the customer kept the item.
notes: Late delivery with explicit money-back language should be refund, not shipping delay.

subject: Where is my order
body: The tracking page still says in transit and the delivery date passed two days ago. Order ORD-3103.
policy_snippet: Shipping delays require the order ID and carrier status before next-step review.
notes: Standard shipping delay should remain supported and not escalate.

subject: Delivered but not here
body: Tracking says my order was delivered but nothing was at my door when I got home. Order ORD-3104.
policy_snippet: Missing order claims require order ID and address confirmation before review.
notes: Delivered-but-not-received should map to missing order handling.

subject: Cancel this order now
body: Please cancel order ORD-3105 before it ships. I placed it by mistake an hour ago.
policy_snippet: Cancellation requests can be reviewed before shipment but are not guaranteed once fulfillment begins.
notes: Should stay cancellation request and keep reply conservative.

subject: Need help with this order
body: I do not want this item anymore. Can I return it or get a refund? Order ORD-3106.
notes: Ambiguous mixed-intent return/refund request should fall back to out of scope for safe human review.

subject: Refund me or I will contact my bank
body: My order arrived late and if you do not refund me today I am contacting my bank and filing a complaint.
customer_history: Customer contacted support twice with no resolution.
notes: Still refund-related, but risk language should escalate.
